question: Comment differencier les etats interactifs (hover, focus, selected, pressed) ?

principles.text: 5 etats distincts: hover (bg subtil 8%), focus (outline 2px+ 3:1 contrast WCAG 2.4.13), pressed (bg plus sombre 10-12%), selected (accent persistent), disabled (38% opacity). :focus-visible pas :focus. M3 state layers model. Chaque etat doit etre visuellement distinct des autres.

sources.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.13 Focus Appearance
sources.quote: Focus indicator must have 3:1 contrast and minimum 2px outline

sources.name: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast
sources.quote: UI components and their states require 3:1 contrast

sources.name: M3 State Layers
sources.quote: State layers use opacity overlays: hover 8%, pressed 10%, dragged 16%

sources.name: NNg Button States
sources.quote: Each interactive state must be visually distinguishable

sources.name: Deque Focus Indicators Guide
sources.quote: Use :focus-visible to avoid showing focus on click

sources.name: Sara Soueidan — Focus Indicators
sources.quote: Custom focus styles must meet WCAG 2.4.13 minimum area
